info.title: Traigent Auth Contract (registered routes)
info.description: Documented Flask auth routes registered in TraigentBackend/src/routes/auth_routes.py. V1: minimal path + method declarations to satisfy the cross-repo conformance gate. Full request/response schemas, JWT field definitions, and error-shape contracts are follow-up authoring work (Batch 3d). Codex review of Batch 3: response codes replaced with `default` until each handler is traced; this prevents the OpenAPI from contradicting actual handler returns.

get.summary: Public JWKS for verifying Traigent-issued JWTs (RFC 7517)

default.description: Response shape pending — see Flask handler in TraigentBackend/src/routes/ for actual return shape; full request/response schemas tracked as follow-up.

get.summary: Get the currently authenticated user's profile

200.description: Authenticated user profile. JWT-backed sessions include data.expires_at.

404.description: Authenticated principal could not be resolved to a user.

put.summary: Update the currently authenticated user's profile

default.description: Response shape pending — see Flask handler in TraigentBackend/src/routes/ for actual return shape; full request/response schemas tracked as follow-up.

get.description: Resolves a tenant by tenant_id or tenant_slug, sets a signed OIDC state cookie, and redirects to the tenant IdP authorization URL.

parameters.name: tenant_id
parameters.description: Tenant id. Either tenant_id or tenant_slug is required by the backend.

parameters.name: tenant_slug
parameters.description: Tenant slug. Either tenant_id or tenant_slug is required by the backend.

parameters.name: return_to
parameters.description: Optional relative return path persisted in OIDC state when it passes backend sanitization.

parameters.name: login_hint
parameters.description: Optional OIDC login_hint forwarded to the IdP.

302.description: Redirect to the tenant OIDC authorization endpoint.

404.description: OIDC SSO is not available for the requested tenant.

get.description: Validates signed state and OIDC identity claims, issues Traigent session tokens, and either returns a JSON login envelope or redirects to the sanitized return_to path.

parameters.name: code
parameters.description: OIDC authorization code. Required for non-error callbacks.

parameters.name: state
parameters.description: Opaque state value matched against the signed OIDC state cookie.

parameters.name: error
parameters.description: IdP error signal. When present, the backend returns OIDC_PROVIDER_REJECTED.

200.description: OIDC login completed and tokens returned as JSON.

X-Session-Expires-At.description: Session expiry as an ISO-8601 UTC timestamp (derived from the JWT exp claim).

302.description: OIDC login completed and the browser was redirected to the sanitized return_to path.

X-Session-Expires-At.description: Session expiry as an ISO-8601 UTC timestamp (derived from the JWT exp claim).

Set-Cookie.description: Browser auth cookies and cleared OIDC state cookie.

400.description: Missing code, invalid state, invalid session id, or invalid tenant OIDC configuration.

403.description: OIDC identity is not allowed for the tenant.

404.description: OIDC SSO is not available for the tenant from signed state.

post.summary: Authenticate with email + password; returns access + refresh tokens

200.description: Authenticated. Session expiry is exposed via the X-Session-Expires-At header, not the body.

X-Session-Expires-At.description: Session expiry as an ISO-8601 UTC timestamp (derived from the JWT exp claim).

post.summary: Invalidate the current session and tokens

default.description: Response shape pending — see Flask handler in TraigentBackend/src/routes/ for actual return shape; full request/response schemas tracked as follow-up.

post.summary: Exchange a refresh token for a new access token

200.description: Refreshed. Session expiry is exposed via the X-Session-Expires-At header, not the body.

X-Session-Expires-At.description: Session expiry as an ISO-8601 UTC timestamp (derived from the JWT exp claim).

get.summary: Fetch a CSRF token for cookie-mode auth (side-effect-free, auth-required)
get.description: Returns a CSRF token and sets it as a non-HttpOnly traigent_csrf_token cookie. The client mirrors the value back in the X-CSRF-Token request header on state-changing requests (double-submit-cookie).

200.description: CSRF token issued (also set as the traigent_csrf_token cookie).

post.summary: Start device authorization for CLI login
post.description: Issues an RFC 8628-style device_code/user_code pair. Portal approval requires a verified logged-in session and is the gate before a project-scoped API key can be issued.

post.summary: Approve or deny a device authorization from the portal
post.description: Session-authenticated portal endpoint called by /activate to record the human decision for a pending device authorization. Uses one decision route with an explicit approved/denied enum so approval and denial share the same user_code validation, auth checks, rate limits, and idempotency semantics. SECURITY INVARIANT: this endpoint MUST NEVER return the API key; the key is delivered exclusively through the CLI's /api/v1/auth/device/token poll.

200.description: Device authorization decision recorded. Response contains only a safe human-facing summary, never the issued API key.

400.description: Invalid decision request, including malformed user_code or unsupported decision value.

401.description: Not authenticated. A logged-in portal session is required to approve or deny device authorization.

403.description: Authenticated portal user is not allowed to decide this device authorization for the resolved project/workspace.

404.description: No pending device authorization exists for the supplied user_code.

409.description: The supplied user_code has already been approved or denied.

410.description: The supplied user_code existed but has expired.

Retry-After.description: Seconds the portal client should wait before retrying.

post.summary: Poll device authorization status and issue a project-scoped API key
post.description: The CLI polls this endpoint with the device_code grant. Success returns a one-time sk_-prefixed API key scoped to the approved default project. Pending/denied/expired/slow_down states use RFC 8628 error names in Traigent's standard error envelope.

200.description: Device authorization approved; project-scoped API key issued.

400.description: Authorization is pending or the device code has expired.

403.description: The human denied the device authorization.

429.description: The client polled faster than the allowed interval.

Retry-After.description: Seconds the CLI should wait before polling again.
